layers.source_url: https://hydro.nationalmap.gov/arcgis/rest/services/nhd/MapServer/6

layers.label: I-93 corridor (front line)
layers.source_url: OpenStreetMap: way[ref="I 93"] within Concord bbox (recut_on_i93.py)

layers.label: East Concord
layers.source_url: derived: city polygon cut on the I-93 corridor

layers.label: West Concord
layers.source_url: derived: city polygon cut on the I-93 corridor

layers.label: Contested zone (river ↔ I-93)
layers.source_url: derived: city pieces between the Merrimack and I-93

layers.label: Contested zone (hatch)
layers.source_url: derived: 150 m / 45° diagonal hatch over the contested zone
layers.note: visual hatching for the contested zone — 1860s campaign-map look

layers.label: Merrimack River crossings
layers.source_url: https://gis.concordnh.gov/arc1061/rest/services/CityGeneral/WaterSystemGIS/MapServer/44

layers.label: East viewshed (3DEP DEM)
layers.source_url: USGS 3DEP DEM + gdal.ViewshedGenerate from airport / armory / Oak Hill
layers.note: Everything East's batteries can SEE — i.e. can shell

layers.label: Beaten zone (West under fire)
layers.source_url: viewshed_east ∩ territory_west — 36.4% of the west bank
layers.note: The west-bank ground actually exposed to East fire

layers.label: Contested flashpoints
layers.source_url: curated points: Hannah Dustin, Exit 17, Sewalls Falls, Exit 16, Fort Eddy plaza, NHTI, Bow Jct

layers.label: HIMARS launchers (3-197 FA)
layers.source_url: NH Army National Guard, 3rd Battalion 197th Field Artillery (M142 HIMARS)
layers.note: Strategic rocket artillery — East's range advantage

layers.label: Black Hawks (AASF / 151st Aviation)
layers.source_url: Army Aviation Support Facility, Concord Municipal Airport
layers.note: UH-60 air mobility — East can bypass the bridges

layers.label: Tanks / tracked armor
layers.source_url: NH National Guard State Military Reservation (reported on-site)
layers.note: East's heaviest ground armor — flips the armor advantage East

layers.label: NHARNG sites
layers.source_url: poster: 07_resources.py (Census geocoded + bank-validated)

layers.label: Police HQ + BearCat (West)
layers.source_url: poster: 07_resources.py

layers.source_url: poster: 07_resources.py

layers.source_url: poster: 07_resources.py

layers.label: Supreme Court / DOS HQ / data ctr
layers.source_url: poster: 07_resources.py

layers.label: Drinking water + WWTPs
layers.source_url: poster: 07_resources.py (Hutchins WTP, Hall St WWTP, Penacook Lake)

layers.label: Garvins Falls hydro (12.4 MW)
layers.source_url: Hull Street Energy / FERC; mid-river on the Bow line
layers.note: The contested generator — neither bank can claim it

layers.source_url: poster: 07_resources.py

layers.source_url: poster: 07_resources.py

layers.label: Power & utility network
layers.source_url: workflow research: substations, Garvins Falls, solar, gas, comms (cited per point)

layers.label: Water & wastewater network
layers.source_url: workflow research: Penacook Lake, Hutchins WTP, Hall St + Penacook WWTP (cited per point)

layers.label: I-93 interchanges
layers.source_url: City of Concord interstate-exit layer (Exits 12-17 = I-93)

layers.label: Blitz — offensive arcs
layers.source_url: authored from the blitz scenario (HIMARS / air assault / armor)
layers.note: East's week-one offensive: bombardment, air assault, armor thrusts

layers.label: 3D terrain (the Heights)
layers.source_url: Mapzen / AWS Terrain Tiles (terrarium raster-DEM)
layers.note: Toggles real 3D relief + hillshade; best with a tilted camera

layers.label: Siege — held/blown crossings
layers.source_url: derived: the crossings as chokepoints West holds or blows

layers.label: City landmarks (187, by category)
layers.source_url: OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL) — strategic named features citywide
layers.note: 187 real named POIs (medical, education, government, power, water, transport, logistics, public safety, worship, comms, fuel, military), banked West/East/Contested.

layers.label: Downtown buildings (3D, lidar height)
layers.source_url: USGS 3DEP lidar (NH_Coastal_2019); nDSM = DSM − ground DTM per OSM footprint
layers.note: 1,273 downtown footprints extruded to their real lidar-derived height — tilt with the 3D button to see the city rise.

layers.label: Forest / woodland
layers.source_url: OpenStreetMap natural=wood / landuse=forest
layers.note: Tree cover — concealment and cover that shapes movement and observation.

layers.label: Lakes, ponds & reservoirs
layers.source_url: City of Concord GIS / USGS NHD

layers.label: Streams & brooks
layers.source_url: USGS NHD flowlines

layers.label: Major roads (arterials)
layers.source_url: OpenStreetMap (motorway/trunk/primary/secondary)
layers.note: The arterial network — how force and supply actually move.

layers.label: Local road network
layers.source_url: OpenStreetMap (tertiary/residential/unclassified)
layers.note: 1,845 local streets — the urban fabric of both banks.

layers.label: Allied towns (by bank)
layers.source_url: derived: longest shared border with West vs East (proximity tiebreak)
layers.note: Each bordering town allies with the bank it shares the most boundary with: West = Bow, Hopkinton, Webster, Boscawen, Dunbarton; East = Pembroke, Chichester, Loudon, Canterbury, Epsom, Allenstown.

layers.label: Neighborhoods under fire
layers.source_url: derived: Voronoi neighborhoods × East viewshed exposure
layers.note: Named neighborhoods shaded by how much of each lies inside the East's line-of-sight fire: heavy / partial / sheltered.

layers.label: Artillery range rings (East battery)
layers.source_url: derived: 2/4/6/8 km from the NHARNG armory firing position
layers.note: Concentric range bands from the East's gun line.
